steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence introduces two examples of ekphrastic poetry: Hayden's poem about Monet, then Fernandez's poem about Klimt. The blank must signal that Fernandez's poem follows the same pattern as Hayden's — a parallel relationship, not a contrast or a cause-and-effect.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "nevertheless,": signals contrast. Fernandez's poem is not a counterexample to Hayden's. ✗
  • "likewise,": means "in the same way" — signals that Fernandez's poem does the same thing as Hayden's (takes a painting as its subject). ✓
  • "hence,": signals a conclusion or result. Fernandez's poem is not caused by or derived from Hayden's. ✗
  • "conversely,": signals an opposite or reversal. The two poems are parallel, not opposite. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
